display.purpose: Hero headlines, marketing surface only. One per page.

headline.purpose: Section headlines. The 'Three legs. One substrate.' voice.

title.purpose: Card titles, smaller heading slots. Same face and weight as headline; difference is scale.

body-large.purpose: Hero subheads and lede copy. The bridge between display and body.

body.purpose: All running copy. Capped at 65 to 75ch line length.

label.purpose: Eyebrows on every section, table column headers, mono utility text. Tracked uppercase signature.

mono.purpose: Inline code, package names, file paths, license abbreviations. Lower-case where source-matched.

shadows.name: button-cue
shadows.purpose: The only shadow in the system. Applied to button-primary as a state cue, not for elevation.

shadows.name: button-cue-hover
shadows.purpose: Hover-state lift on button-primary. Barely-perceptible, tinted toward sea-ink.

shadows.name: mark-shadow-on-dark
shadows.purpose: Filter applied to the brand mark on the closing-band atoll gradient. Not a box-shadow; an SVG drop-shadow filter for legibility against the gradient.

motion.name: ease-tide
motion.purpose: The tide easing. Glacial. Used only on the 8s brand-mark hover rotation.

motion.name: ease-swell
motion.purpose: Default state-change easing. 180ms duration. Hover, focus, simple transitions.

motion.name: duration-tide
motion.purpose: Brand mark hover rotation duration. Suppressed under prefers-reduced-motion.

patterns.name: pattern-topo
patterns.value: url("data:image/svg+xml;utf8,%3Csvg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 240 240'%3E%3Cg fill='none' stroke='%23173a40' stroke-width='1'%3E%3Ccircle cx='120' cy='120' r='30'/%3E%3Ccircle cx='120' cy='120' r='58'/%3E%3Ccircle cx='120' cy='120' r='86'/%3E%3Ccircle cx='120' cy='120' r='118'/%3E%3Ccircle cx='120' cy='120' r='154'/%3E%3C/g%3E%3C/svg%3E")
patterns.purpose: Concentric ring tile in sea-ink stroke. Used at low opacity (6 to 8 percent) behind feature grids. The mark concept echoes this motif compressed into a single mark.

patterns.name: pattern-topo-light
patterns.value: url("data:image/svg+xml;utf8,%3Csvg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 240 240'%3E%3Cg fill='none' stroke='%23f6fefb' stroke-width='1'%3E%3Ccircle cx='120' cy='120' r='30'/%3E%3Ccircle cx='120' cy='120' r='58'/%3E%3Ccircle cx='120' cy='120' r='86'/%3E%3Ccircle cx='120' cy='120' r='118'/%3E%3Ccircle cx='120' cy='120' r='154'/%3E%3C/g%3E%3C/svg%3E")
patterns.purpose: Foam-light variant. Used over atoll/lagoon gradients with mix-blend-mode: screen at 16 to 18 percent opacity.

gradients.name: gradient-lagoon
gradients.purpose: Lagoon to lagoon-deep. The brand mark fill on light surfaces. Load-bearing; the mark IS this gradient.

gradients.name: gradient-atoll
gradients.purpose: Lagoon-deep to palm. The closing-band substrate. Inverted-section ground for any dark band on the marketing surface.

breakpoints.name: tablet
breakpoints.purpose: Desktop to tablet collapse. Hero spec strips collapse to 2 columns; feature grids reflow to 1.

breakpoints.name: mobile
breakpoints.purpose: Tablet to mobile. Section padding-y compresses; brand-lockup and pill stack.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Filled sea-ink primary action. The single drop-shadow exception in the system as a state cue.

components.name: On-Dark Primary Button
components.description: Foam-light fill primary for atoll-gradient surfaces. Filled sea-ink on the gradient midpoint fails AA contrast; this is the AA-safe inversion. Closing-band primary slot only.

components.name: Ghost on Dark Button
components.description: Foam-light translucent secondary. Only on atoll-gradient or lagoon-gradient surfaces.

components.name: Quiet Link
components.description: Transparent secondary CTA with a translating arrow. Hero secondary slot and courtesy off-ramps.

components.name: Status Pill
components.description: Foam-grounded lagoon-stroked label. Static; never animated, never hovered. Used as the eyebrow above the hero headline.
components.css: .ds-pill { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 4px 12px; border-radius: 999px; background: #f3faf5; color: #328f97; border: 1px solid rgba(50, 143, 151, 0.32); font-family: "Manrope", ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; }

components.name: Tag Chip
components.description: Inline binding label inside feature cards. Mono and tinted; static.
components.css: .ds-tag { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 3px 8px; border-radius: 4px; background: rgba(50, 143, 151, 0.10); color: #328f97; font-family: "JetBrains Mono", ui-monospace, "SF Mono", Menlo, Consolas, monospace; font-weight: 500; font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.45; letter-spacing: 0.02em; }

components.name: Feature Card
components.description: Ledger card. Eyebrow, title, body, dashed-rule tag row. Hairline rules instead of shadows.
components.html:
Evolving

Memory that updates without forgetting

A new fact supersedes the old one on the same topic and keeps the history. Ingest is idempotent; forget is explicit.

supersession topic_key

components.name: Brand Lockup (Light)
components.description: Brand mark plus wordmark. Inline SVG via use; gradient variant on light surfaces, slow tide-rotation on hover.
components.html: Memoturn

components.name: Brand Lockup (Dark)
components.description: Mono brand mark on sea-ink or atoll-gradient surfaces. currentColor inheritance via fill.
components.html: Memoturn

narrative.overview: Memoturn is a memory database for AI agents, and the visual system says so before any copy is read. The wedge is durable, evolving memory (typed facts superseding by topic, whole profiles branching in O(1)); the design system is built to feel that way: tight headlines that read as terminal output, hairline rules that read as ledger structure, a slow tide rotation on the brand mark that registers more like a horizon shift than a UI animation. Restraint carries it. One radius, two type faces (plus mono for code), four button shapes, a single load-bearing gradient. The discipline is the brand. Memoturn deliberately rejects every standard AI-tool aesthetic: no generic SaaS gradient-hero (Cursor, Vercel), no neon-purple-on-black (OpenAI, Replit), no monospace-brutalist (HN-aesthetic), no enterprise-AI navy-and-rounded (IBM watsonx, Azure-AI-Foundry), and no AI-agent orchestrator glow (the saturating family in the agent-memory / agent-platform space, deliberately unnamed because it includes Memoturn's direct competitors: glassmorphic tiles on charcoal, pulsing cyan/teal accent borders, agent avatars with glow halos, "AI inbox" three-column layouts). The brand's job is to find a sixth lane. Memoturn is the closest neighbor by category to the orchestrator-glow family, so the visual distance from it has to be the largest.
keyCharacteristics: One radius (6px). One heading face (Inter Tight 700). One body face (Manrope 400). One mono stack (JetBrains Mono).
Four named button shapes, no fifth: filled sea-ink primary (light surfaces), on-dark primary (foam-light fill on atoll/lagoon gradients), ghost-on-dark, quiet-link.
Lagoon to Atoll gradient is the only gradient; it is load-bearing on the brand mark and the closing band, never decorative.
Hairline rules and tonal layering carry depth. Surface shadows are forbidden; one tiny button drop-shadow exists as a state cue, not as elevation.

The database is the demo. Live indicators (a recent-memory ticker, a tail of ingest/recall/supersede/branch events, measured-latency receipts) earn their place by being the proof; static product screenshots do not.

rules.name: The Lagoon-Atoll Rule
rules.body: The two named gradients (gradient-lagoon: lagoon to lagoon-deep, and gradient-atoll: lagoon-deep to palm) are the only gradients permitted in the system. Any other gradient is forbidden. The lagoon gradient is the brand mark fill; the atoll gradient is the inverted band substrate. Load-bearing, never decorative.

rules.name: The Ember Cap Rule
rules.body: Ember (#e89456) covers less than 5% of any composition. Use it for chart accents, license-table proprietary-row markers, and occasional state cues. Forbidden as a CTA fill, as a body text color, or as a section ground.

rules.name: The Page-White Choice
rules.body: The page substrate is pure #ffffff, not a tinted near-white. The cool neutrals (foam, sand, foam-light) carry the lagoon-tinted warmth; the page itself stays neutral so the lagoon gradient and the brand mark register at full saturation.

rules.name: The Foam-Light-On-Dark Rule
rules.body: On any surface darker than the lagoon stops (sea-ink, atoll gradient, lagoon gradient), the only valid foreground is foam-light (#f6fefb). Never use the gradient mark on these surfaces; switch to the mono variant with foam-light fill.

rules.name: The Tight-Set Rule
rules.body: Headlines use line-height 1.02 to 1.05 and letter-spacing -0.025em (display) or -0.02em (title). Loosening these for breathing room is forbidden. The tight set is what makes Inter Tight read as a machine voice, not as a magazine.

rules.name: The Em-Dash Exception
rules.body: Memoturn copy uses spaced em-dashes in headlines and CTAs as a brand rhythm. This is a documented exception to the shared design law. Substituting hyphens or other punctuation in interface copy is forbidden. The em-dash carries the brand voice.

rules.name: The Two-Face Rule
rules.body: The system uses Inter Tight (heading) and Manrope (body) plus JetBrains Mono (code/labels). A third typeface is forbidden. Italics are not part of the system; any italic copy is a flag to revise.

rules.name: The Full-Ink Body Rule
rules.body: Body copy on light surfaces uses full foreground ink (#252525), not the muted variant. Muted is for eyebrows, captions, and metadata only. Softening body to muted is forbidden.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Resting surfaces (cards, sections, banners, callouts, panels) have no shadow. Depth is achieved via hairline rules, ground tones, and content rhythm. Overlays (modal, dialog, popover, sheet, tooltip, hover-card, dropdown content) are exempt: they get a functional shadow because elevation IS their meaning.

rules.name: The Hairline Rule
rules.body: 1px hairlines at sea-ink 16% alpha (#173a4029, the rule token) carry structure inside cards, tables, and ledger grids. Distinct from border (#ebebeb) which is for component containment.

rules.name: The Tonal-Step Rule
rules.body: Backgrounds run page (#ffffff) to foam (#f3faf5) to sand (#e7f0e8). Each step adds slight cool warmth and subtracts a hair of luminance. Depth comes from the step itself; never add a shadow on top of the step.

rules.name: The Four-Shapes Rule
rules.body: Four button shapes total: filled sea-ink primary (light surfaces), on-dark primary (foam-light fill on atoll/lagoon gradients), ghost-on-dark (atoll/lagoon gradient secondary), quiet-link (any surface secondary). A fifth shape is a flag to revise; if a fifth seems necessary, the design wanted a different component.

rules.name: The Inline-Mark Rule
rules.body: The brand mark is rendered via inline svg and use. CSS mask-image: url(...) and img src are both forbidden in production because they fail differently: mask-image fails silently on file:// and in restrictive sandboxes; img cannot inherit currentColor.

rules.name: The Card-Grid-Three-Only Rule
rules.body: Identical card grids are forbidden by the shared design law. Memoturn permits a single exception: the three-leg feature grid. Three is the only valid count; four-up grids and six-up grids are forbidden.

dos: Do lead with mechanism. Name the primitive (profile, typed memory, burner branch, segment, writer lease), the guarantee (supersession, idempotent ingest, txid, epoch fencing), the surface (HTTP, MCP, @memoturn/sdk, the memoturn CLI), the artifact (MEMOTURN_OBJECT_STORE, the Helm chart). Specificity is what the Agent Builder trusts.
Do hold the page substrate pure white (#ffffff). The neutrals (foam, sand) carry the warmth.
Do use the lagoon (gradient-lagoon) and atoll (gradient-atoll) gradients only as the brand mark fill and the inverted band substrate. They are load-bearing, not decorative.
Do keep headlines tight: line-height 1.02 to 1.05, letter-spacing -0.025em to -0.02em. The tight set is the brand voice.
Do use spaced em-dashes in headlines and CTAs (Get started — free). The em-dash is a documented brand exception to the shared no-em-dash law; it carries voice.
Do render the brand mark inline via symbol and use. Never via CSS mask-image and never via img src.
Do use foam-light (#f6fefb) as the only foreground on sea-ink, atoll gradient, and lagoon gradient surfaces.

Do show the database working somewhere on every page. Mechanical verbs (ingested, superseded, rewound) in copy; tiny live indicators (recent-memory ticker, measured-latency receipts) in UI. Static product screenshots do not earn their place.

Do use the four button shapes only: filled sea-ink primary (light surfaces), on-dark primary (foam-light fill on atoll/lagoon gradients), ghost-on-dark (atoll/lagoon gradient secondary), quiet-link (any surface secondary). A fifth shape is a flag to revise the design.
Do honor prefers-reduced-motion on the 8s tide rotation. The mark may still color-shift on hover; the rotation is suppressed.

Do test the category-reflex check at both altitudes. The first-order answer (domain to palette) must come back ambiguous. The second-order answer (domain plus anti-references to aesthetic family) must also come back ambiguous.
donts: Don't look like generic SaaS gradient-hero (Cursor.com, Vercel-style). Big gradient hero, drop-shadowed product screenshot, identical 3-icon feature cards, Trusted-by YC-logo strip. The dominant cliche of the category.
Don't look like AI-product neon-purple-on-black (OpenAI, Replit-style). Cyberpunk dark mode, glowing edges, neural-net imagery, the-future-of-coding positioning.
Don't look like developer-tool brutalist-monospace (HN-aesthetic). All-monospace, terminal-green-on-black, ASCII art, NO-JS banners.
Don't look like enterprise-AI navy-and-rounded (IBM watsonx, Azure-AI-Foundry-style). Navy blue, oversized rounded corners, stock photo of a hand on a glass touchscreen.

Don't look like AI-agent orchestrator glow (the saturating family in the agent-memory / agent-platform space, deliberately unnamed because it includes Memoturn's direct competitors). Glassmorphic tiles on a near-black or charcoal panel, pulsing animated accent borders in cyan / magenta / teal, agent avatars or cards with glow halos, faux-3D depth on every tile, agent-activity feeds with status dots, three-column AI-inbox layouts. Memoturn is this family's nearest neighbor by category, so the visual distance must be the largest of any anti-reference. If the two surfaces could be confused at a glance, redesign.
Don't use border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent on cards, alerts, or callouts. Side-stripe borders are forbidden by the shared design law.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text on a gradient background). Use a single solid color. Emphasis through weight or scale only.

Don't apply box-shadows to resting surfaces (cards, sections, banners, callouts, panels). The system is flat by default. The 1px button-cue is the only shadow on a resting surface; overlays (modal, dialog, popover, sheet, tooltip, hover-card, dropdown content) are exempt because elevation IS their meaning.
Don't add a fifth button shape. The four named shapes (filled sea-ink primary, on-dark primary, ghost-on-dark, quiet-link) are the spec. A fifth shape means the design wanted a different component.
Don't soften body copy to muted (#878787). Body uses full ink (foreground). Muted is for eyebrows, captions, metadata.
Don't introduce a third typeface. Inter Tight (heading) plus Manrope (body) plus JetBrains Mono (code/labels) is the system. Italics are not part of the system.
Don't put coastal vocabulary (tide, lagoon, atoll, swell, foam, sand) in interface copy. The metaphor lives in token names; the prose stays in product language.
Don't use hype words: revolutionary, next-generation, cutting-edge, game-changing, unlock, empower, seamlessly, leverage, best-in-class.
Don't use exclamation marks in marketing copy.
Don't use emojis in marketing surface copy. CLI status output may use one of triangle, anchor, check if functional. Nothing else.
Don't invent additional gradients. The lagoon and atoll gradients are the spec. A radial blur, a cyan-magenta wash, or a dark hero overlay is forbidden.
Don't use ember (#e89456) as a CTA fill or section ground. Ember is capped at less than 5% of any composition.
Don't rely on color alone for status. Every status carries a textual or shape redundancy.
Don't ship a card grid with more than three cells.
